mnemonics.title: BFS vs DFS Memory Aid
mnemonics.content:
BFS: Queue (Breadth-First Search) explores level by level
DFS: Stack (Depth-First Search) goes deep then backtracks

mnemonics.title: Graph Types Quick Recall
mnemonics.content:
Complete: Everyone knows everyone
Bipartite: Two teams, no friends within team
Cyclic: Round and round
Acyclic: No circles allowed

acronyms.usage: Graph traversal, cycle detection, topological sort
Shortest path in unweighted graphs, level-order traversal
Network design, connecting all points with minimum cost
Task scheduling, dependency resolution, version control

visualTips.visualization: Think of a chessboard/sudoku grid where rows and columns are vertices. Put 1 where edges exist.
Think of a party where each person (vertex) has a list of friends (neighbors) they talked to.
Imagine a tree trunk connecting all branches without creating loops - minimal connections keeping everything attached.
Think of a dating app: two groups (males/females) only match across groups, never within.

analogies.analogy: Like people (vertices) and friendships (edges) in a social circle.
Like road distances on a map - the weight represents how long/far it takes to travel.
BFS is like ripples in water spreading outward; DFS is like exploring a maze by going deep before backtracking.
Like finding the fastest route on Google Maps - consider traffic and distance.
Like islands in an ocean - each component is a group of connected land masses.
